key_insight: Chemical disorder and local chemical ordering in WTaCrV change radiation damage pathways: both increase primary defects relative to a homogenized average-atom model, yet they help preserve mechanical strength under irradiation.
engineering_relevance: For nuclear structural materials, capturing nanoscale chemical heterogeneity is essential because it can improve irradiation tolerance and limit strength loss in refractory high-entropy alloys.
method: Compare three chemistries: average-atom (AA), random solid-solution (RSS), and local chemical order (LCO)
Use newly developed interatomic potentials to quantify Frenkel-pair production and mechanical response
evidence_signal: The abstract reports Frenkel-pair counts following NRSS > NLCO > NAA at the same dose, while irradiation strongly degrades AA but has limited impact on strength and flow stress in RSS and LCO models.
watch_item: The conclusions rely on atomistic modeling and specific interatomic potentials, so predictive accuracy depends on how well those potentials capture real irradiation and defect kinetics.
paper_title: Primary damage and mechanical degradation of WTaCrV refractory high-entropy alloy: effects of solid-solution and chemical ordering
